pages.title: Three Folder System Head Hands Heart
pages.body:
# The 3-Folder System: Atlas → Projects → End Products (Head, Hands, Heart)

**Source:** YouTube — "Your file structure is a map of your cognitive architecture"
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Rl7rvHbDqGk

## Summary

The speaker discovered that disorganized AI outputs were a reflection of disorganized thinking. After months of building AI agents and burning API tokens, he stopped and realized the real problem wasn't the tools — it was his cognitive architecture. His core insight: **your file structure is a map of your thinking**. Most people's maps are a junk drawer with a search bar.

## The Three-Folder Framework

### 1. Atlas (Head / Thinking)
Raw materials, ideas, strategy, capture. Where you put ideas when you get them — the thinking layer.

### 2. Projects (Hands / Building)
Active work. Content being created, things being built.

### 3. End Products (Heart / Serving)
Completed work. Finished deliverables that serve other people.

## Key Insights
- **File structure = cognitive architecture.**
- **Organization is speed.** Clear structure makes AI faster and cheaper (fewer tokens).
- **The sophistication trap.** Smart people overcomplicate things.
- **AI reveals you to yourself.** AI externalizes your thinking patterns.
- **Agents mirror human growth.** An agent needs identity, context, and skills.

pages.title: Nick Milo
pages.body: Obsidian and second-brain advocate. Known for promoting simple folder structures and the Linking Your Thinking (LYT) framework. His patterns around three-folder organization influenced the Head, Hands, Heart system. Referenced in the three-folder system concept for his Obsidian/second-brain approaches.

pages.title: Jake Van Cleef
pages.body: Author of an academic paper arguing to stop building AI agents and focus on file folder structure instead. His work was a key influence on the Three Folder System / Head Hands Heart framework. The paper was processed through Claude for plain-English explanation.

pages.title: Bob Proctor
pages.body: Motivational speaker, author, philosopher. Known for the quote "Organization is heaven's first law," referenced in the Three Folder System framework video as the core organizing principle.

pages.title: Meeting with TJ van der Molen
pages.body:
**Date:** 2026-06-05
**Attendees:** TJ van der Molen, sayf

Met with TJ van der Molen, CEO and ECHA Specialist at Passend. Discussed his business progress, partnerships, and growth plans. Explored how Maloha Coast can support Passend's development needs.

## Three Projects Discussed

### 1. Database + Frontend — Project-Based Learning Showcase
Chronological records of student projects & learning outcomes. Distribute updates via portal/newsletters/social media.

### 2. Internal Tool — Reporting Automation
Rapid data input tools for behavioral observations with database storage and report generation. Staff verification for AI/OCR data.

### 3. SaaS Platform — Counselor Teaching & Learning
Freemium to subscription. Three tiers: Course access, Curriculum sharing, Hosted events. Certification at all levels.

## Next Steps
- Requirements Engineering Workshop: MYR 100/hr or MYR 800/day
- TJ to prioritize which system to build first

pages.title: Passend
pages.body:
Malaysian education platform led by TJ van der Molen (CEO & ECHA Specialist). Three-product pipeline: project-based learning showcase, reporting automation, and SaaS counselor platform. Early-stage prospect for Maloha Coast.

## Product Pipeline
1. **Database + Frontend — Project-Based Learning Showcase:** Chronological records via portal/newsletters/social media
2. **Internal Tool — Reporting Automation:** Behavioral observation tools for teachers
3. **SaaS Platform — Counselor Teaching and Learning:** Freemium to subscription, three tiers

## Open Threads
- TJ to prioritize first product
- Workshop proposed: MYR 100/hr or MYR 800/day
- sayf's availability open until May, then contract work begins

pages.title: TJ van der Molen
pages.body:
CEO & ECHA Specialist at Passend. Met June 2026 to discuss three potential development projects. Early-stage prospect — interest confirmed, awaiting prioritization decision.

## What They're Building
Passend — education platform: learning showcase, reporting automation, SaaS counselor platform.

## Assessment
Early-stage prospect. Three distinct systems identified — sensible to prioritize one for MVP. Clear vision for Passend's growth. Workshop will reveal more about decision-making style and budget.

## Relationship
- 2026-06-05: Initial meeting — discussed business progress and three projects
- Next step: TJ to prioritize which system to build first

pages.title: Agent Hierarchy
pages.body:
The Maloha Coast Hermes ecosystem has three agents in a hierarchy:

**falconhermes** (root) — Orchestrator. Sayf's primary interface. Delegates tasks to MC and Sammy. Handles strategy, high-level planning.

**MC** (profiles/mc/) — Executive assistant. Inbox triage, calendar, project tracking, client communications, research, business operations.

**Sammy** (profiles/sammy/) — Content creator. LinkedIn posts, X threads, blog articles, brand voice, engagement drafting.

Backed up to github.com/Maloha-Coast/hermes-profile-backup.

pages.title: Cold Lead Response — Conference Events
pages.body:
Response to a conference producer who asked for examples after cold outreach. Crafted for LinkedIn DM — punchy, concrete, pilot-friendly.

## Use Cases Pitched

**Speaker sourcing:** Scans hundreds of profiles, past talks, decks → shortlist + briefing notes in ~15 minutes.

**Post-session repurposing:** Recording/transcript → LinkedIn article, 3-5 social posts, newsletter summary, key highlights. One session → 30+ usable pieces.

**Sponsor deliverables:** Pre-event copy, staff briefs, lead reports, post-event ROI summaries — cutting out ~20 hours per sponsor tier.

**Business brain / gbrain:** Agent learns past events, speakers, sponsors. Ask "What sponsors performed best last year?" or "Give me the checklist for a 500-person event" — pulls from your own history.

No AI jargon — pitched as a tool they control. Each example maps to a known pain point for conference producers.

pages.title: VPS Hardening
pages.body:
Security hardening of the Maloha Coast production VPS (Ubuntu, 6.8.0-124-generic).

## SSH Hardening
- PermitRootLogin: prohibit-password
- PasswordAuthentication: no
- X11Forwarding: no
- MaxAuthTries: 3
- ClientAliveInterval: 300s
- AllowUsers: ubuntu only

## Firewall (UFW)
- Default: deny incoming, allow outgoing
- Open: 22/tcp (SSH) only

## fail2ban
- Jail: sshd (aggressive mode)
- Max retries: 3, Ban time: 1 hour
- Banned 4 IPs within 2 minutes of coming online

pages.title: Gateway Infrastructure
pages.body:
## Hardware
2GB VPS (Tencent Cloud, Ubuntu, 6.8.0-124). 2GB swap. ~50GB storage.

## Hermes Gateway
Systemd user service. Auto-starts on boot. Linger enabled.

### Memory Profile
- Baseline: ~97MB RSS
- After 14h moderate use: ~600MB (growth ~36MB/h)
- Peak observed: 1.0G

### Health Watchdog
Python script running every 10 minutes via cron. Silent when healthy; alerts Discord #Home on threshold breaches:
- Gateway RSS >= 1.4GB (warn) / >= 1.6GB (critical)
- Free memory <= 300MB (warn) / <= 150MB (critical)
- Swap > 200MB
- Memory growth > 50MB/hour

pages.title: Maloha Coast
pages.body:
Maloha Coast Sdn Bhd — AI consulting and development agency bringing AI to SMBs locally and worldwide.

## Director
Sayf (username: falcon)

## Infrastructure
- VPS hardened and secured
- Google Workspace configured (info@malohacoast.com)
- gbrain knowledge base with embeddings enabled
- Hermes MC profile as admin assistant

## Brand
- Primary: #2a6a8a (teal blue), Accent: #c4a86a (champagne gold)
- Optima font
- Gold-on-navy honeycomb hex pattern

pages.title: Maloha Coast — Brand Identity
pages.body:
## Colour Palette
- Primary: #2a6a8a (teal blue)
- Accent: #c4a86a (champagne gold)
- Dark Navy: #0a1628
- Light backgrounds: #f4f8fb, #ffffff

## Typography
- Primary: Optima (humanist, elegant)
- Fallback: 'Segoe UI', 'Candara', 'Palatino Linotype', Palatino, serif

## Brand Pattern
Honeycomb Hexagon Grid — Gold on Navy. Tile: 90×52 px seamless PNG.

## Email Templates
Production template based on "Ocean Deep" direction. Outer wrapper with gold-on-navy hex pattern. White rounded body card. Gold accent borders. Gmail-compatible (HTML background attribute, inline styles, max 600px width).

pages.title: Embedding Cost Projection
pages.body:
Model: text-embedding-3-large ($0.00013 / 1K tokens)

## Per-Page Average
~772 tokens/page → ~$0.0001/page

## Cost Projection
| Pages | Cost |
|-------|------|
| 100 | $0.01 |
| 1,000 | $0.10 |
| 10,000 | $1.00 |
| 100,000 | $10.00 |

Key insight: The real bottleneck is content curation, not API pricing. Even War and Peace (~775K tokens) costs ~$0.10.

pages.title: Hermes Profile Backup
pages.body:
Pushes all agent profiles to private GitHub repo with secrets stripped.

## Repository
- github.com/Maloha-Coast/hermes-profile-backup (private)
- 1,186 files across 2 profiles (default + MC)
- Includes: SOUL.md, skills, cron, memories, config.yaml (stripped)
- Excludes: API keys, tokens, session logs, OAuth tokens

## Restore
git clone → ./setup.sh → edit config.yaml and .env with credentials

pages.title: gbrain
pages.body:
gbrain v0.42.10.0 — local AI-powered knowledge base for Hermes agent.

## Setup
- CLI: ~/.bun/bin/gbrain
- Brain: ~/.gbrain/brain.pglite (PGLite — local Postgres)
- Config: ~/.gbrain/config.json
- Schema: gbrain-base-v2

## Models
| Role | Model |
|------|-------|
| Embedding | openai:text-embedding-3-large (1536d) |
| Query Expansion | anthropic:claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 |
| Chat | anthropic:claude-opus-4-7 |

## Status
- ✅ Keyword search works
- ✅ 49 skills loaded
- ✅ Embeddings enabled (100% coverage)

pages.title: June 3, 2026 — Setup Day
pages.body:
## Highlights
- Full VPS security audit and hardening
- Google Workspace admin setup (info@malohacoast.com)
- gbrain installation and configuration
- Obsidian vault creation

## VPS Hardening
SSH locked down, UFW active, fail2ban running — banned 4 brute-force IPs. New kernel 6.8.0-124 active after reboot.

## AI Infrastructure
gbrain v0.42.10.0 installed, brain initialized at ~/.gbrain/. Hermes agent configured with 49 skills.
